message:
test(mcp/conformance): add wa.workflow_run execute-path snapshots (ft-l0am8)

Fresh-eyes review of 01f690bf flagged that ft-8pcyz pinned envelopes
for 6 wa.* tools but `wa.workflow_run` only had a dry-run snapshot
(`mcp_conformance_additional_tools__wa_workflow_run_conformance.snap`).
The real-execute path (mcp_tools.rs:3025-3070 — find_workflow_by_name +
runner.run_workflow + status mapping over WorkflowExecutionResult)
had no golden artifact, so a refactor of the runner could silently
change the operator-visible response shape and the conformance suite
would let it through.

Two new snapshots cover the execute-path matrix:

* wa_workflow_run_execute_path
    Calls `wa.workflow_run` with `dry_run: false` against the real
    built-in workflow `handle_usage_limits` on a pane whose state
    doesn't satisfy the workflow's preconditions. The runner gets
    far enough to attempt `send_ctrl_c` (the workflow's first
    destructive step), the policy gate denies it under the harness's
    default safety config, and the response surfaces the structured
    `FT-MCP-0008` envelope with a deterministic error message
    ("Failed to exit Codex: Ctrl-C requires approval: Destructive
    action 'send_ctrl_c' requires approval"). This pins the runner →
    policy → envelope path end-to-end.

* wa_workflow_run_execute_unknown_name
    Calls `wa.workflow_run` with `dry_run: false` on a workflow name
    that isn't registered. mcp_tools.rs:3027-3036 returns the
    `Workflow 'X' not found` envelope with the watch-mode hint —
    fully deterministic, no execution_id, no timing. Pins the
    operator-visible "this workflow doesn't exist" contract.

Volatile fields handled by extending `canonicalize`:

* `execution_id` — the runner generates `format!("mcp-{name}-{now_ms}")`
  per invocation (mcp_tools.rs:3038). Replaced with the stable
  placeholder "[execution_id]" so the snapshot freezes the SHAPE of
  the field (non-empty string when execute path runs) without the
  timestamp churn. Canonicalize already handled `now`, `elapsed_ms`,
  `last_refreshed_at`, and any `*_ms` suffix — adding `execution_id`
  is consistent with that pattern. No other current snapshot
  references `execution_id`, so the canonicalize change is local in
  effect.

The new tests use a small `WorkflowRunExecuteCapture` struct and a
matching `workflow_execute_snapshot` helper that captures the FT-MCP
envelope's full shape (ok / data / error / error_code / hint /
scenario / tool) so error-path snapshots have the same structural
floor as success-path ones.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-core \
      --test mcp_conformance_additional_tools \
      --features mcp,asupersync-runtime workflow_run

The first execution (under INSTA_UPDATE=always) populated both new
snapshots with the canonicalized envelope content shown above; the
existing dry-run snapshot is unchanged because dry_run data has no
execution_id. The unknown-workflow envelope is fully deterministic;
the execute-path envelope is deterministic under the harness's default
config (policy gate decides on `send_ctrl_c` regardless of pane
contents). No production change.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+---
+source: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_additional_tools.rs
+assertion_line: 731
+expression: workflow_execute_snapshot(&capture)
+---
+{
+  "data": null,
+  "error": "Failed to exit Codex: Ctrl-C requires approval: Destructive action 'send_ctrl_c' requires approval",
+  "error_code": "FT-MCP-0008",
+  "hint": null,
+  "ok": false,
+  "scenario": "execute_real_workflow_no_preconditions",
+  "tool": "wa.workflow_run"
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+---
+source: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_additional_tools.rs
+expression: workflow_execute_snapshot(&capture)
+---
+{
+  "data": null,
+  "error": "Workflow 'this_workflow_does_not_exist_in_any_registry' not found",
+  "error_code": "FT-MCP-0008",
+  "hint": "Ensure workflows are enabled or run ft watch for event-driven workflows.",
+  "ok": false,
+  "scenario": "execute_unknown_workflow_name",
+  "tool": "wa.workflow_run"
+}
